claim.content: For least-squares fitting of computed quasiparticle energies ε(k) versus momentum magnitude k obtained from finite-N quantum Monte Carlo simulations of the 2D-UEL when more than twenty k values in the interval 0 < k < 1.7 k_F are sampled, the quartic polynomial form ε(k) = a_0 + a_2 k^2 + a_4 k^4 produces more stable fits (smaller residuals and more reproducible derivative at k_F across finite-N datasets) than the Padé rational form ε(k) = (a_0 + a_1 k + a_2 k^2 + a_3 k^3)/(1 + 2 a_3 k), and the quartic fits yield slightly larger values of the extracted effective mass m* = k_F · (dε/dk)^{-1}_{k_F} for the datasets considered in the study, making the quartic choice a practically preferable and systematically biasing choice for the authors' finite-N QMC datasets in the examined parameter range.
